title: achillea lanulosa subsp. alpicola
intro: Achillea lanulosa subsp. alpicola, also known as alpine yarrow, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family. It is commonly found in mountainous regions of North America and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant's leaves and flowers contain compounds with antimicrobial, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating a variety of health issues, including digestive problems, headaches, and respiratory issues. Achillea lanulosa subsp. alpicola can be prepared into teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized in various herbal remedies.
